_comment_header: Sinister Mesh ACL template :: Author: RKOJ-ELENO :: 2026-05-24
_comment_status: SCAFFOLDED — paste into https://login.tailscale.com/admin/acls after generating tags.
_comment_intent: Operator: 'each one of my servers is a complete ghost' + 'all routing needs to pristine and efficient'.

acls._comment: Operator devices (laptop, phone, dev box) can reach every fleet surface on the tailnet.
Servers can mesh-talk to each other (replication, CRDT sync, vault sync) on the well-known fleet ports.
Agents (containerized claude / bot processes on a node) can talk to local-node servers but not cross-server unless their lane needs it.

ssh._comment: Operator can SSH into server-tagged nodes without password (Tailscale SSH; no separate sshd config needed).

nodeAttrs._comment: Force ephemeral devices to use Tailscale's DERP relays if direct UDP fails (covers coffee-shop wifi).

_apply_steps: 1. Generate auth keys at https://login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/keys (one per node; mark NY+FL keys as 'reusable' if you want survival across re-installs).
2. Paste THIS file into https://login.tailscale.com/admin/acls (Tailscale strips _comment* fields automatically).
3. Tag each node when it joins: 'tailscale up --authkey=tskey-... --advertise-tags=tag:server' on NY+FL, 'tag:operator' on laptop/phone.
4. Verify with 'tailscale status' that tags landed; then 'tailscale ping <peer>' for each fleet pair.
